syntax_12575.creator: J__00
syntax_12575.title: Block - can Be Waterlogged
syntax_12575.description: Checks whether or not one or more blocks, blockstates or blockdatas can be waterlogged.

syntax_12576.creator: J__00
syntax_12576.title: Block - is Full Block
syntax_12576.description:
Checks if a block is a full block (like dirt, stone, endstone).
Essentially checks if a block is 1x1x1 and only has one mesh.
syntax_12576.syntax_pattern:
%blocks/blockstates% (is|are) [a] full block[s]
%blocks/blockstates% (isn't|is not|aren't|are not) [a] full block[s]

en:
Checks if a block is a full block (like dirt, stone, endstone).
Essentially checks if a block is 1x1x1 and only has one mesh.

syntax_12578.creator: J__00
syntax_12578.title: BlockState - is Placed
syntax_12578.description:
Checks whether the provided blockstates are placed in a world, if not, they are 'virtual' (e. g. on an item).
Some syntaxes will not work if the block state isn't placed in the world.

en:
Checks whether the provided blockstates are placed in a world, if not, they are 'virtual' (e. g. on an item).

syntax_12589.creator: J__00
syntax_12589.title: Entity - is Aggressive
syntax_12589.description:

Some mobs will raise their arm(s) when aggressive:
Drowned, Piglin, Skeleton, Zombie, ZombieVillager, Illusioner, Vindicator, Panda, Pillager, PiglinBrute

Note: This doesn't always return the actual aggressive state as when set, Pandas are always aggressive if their combined Panda Gene is AGGRESSIVE.

en:

syntax_12598.creator: J__00
syntax_12598.title: Entity/Block/BlockState/Item - is Collidable
syntax_12598.description:
Checks whether the provided living entities, blocks, blockstates and items are collidable.

Info:
- LivingEntity: Some entities might be exempted from the collidable rule of this entity. Use the "Entity - Collidable Exemptions" expression to get these. Please note that this method returns only the custom collidable state, not whether the entity is non-collidable for other reasons such as being dead.

en:
Checks whether the provided living entities, blocks, blockstates and items are collidable.

Info:
- LivingEntity: Some entities might be exempted from the collidable rule of this entity. Use the "Entity - Collidable Exemptions" expression to get these. Please note that this method returns only the custom collidable state, not whether the entity is non-collidable for other reasons such as being dead.

syntax_12599.creator: J__00
syntax_12599.title: Fox - Trusts Players

en: Checks if the provided foxes trust the provided offline players.

syntax_9115.creator: J__00
syntax_9115.title: Goat - Has Left/Right/Both/Either Horn
syntax_9115.description: Checks if goat has the left, right, both or either horn.
syntax_9115.syntax_pattern:
%livingentities% (has|have) [its|the[ir]] (left|right|either|any) [goat] horn
%livingentities% (doesn't|does not|do not|don't) have [its|the[ir]] (left|right|either|any) [goat] horn
%livingentities% (has|have) [its|the[ir]|both] [goat] horns
%livingentities% (doesn't|does not|do not|don't) have [its|the[ir]|both] [goat] horns

en: Checks if goat has the left, right, both or either horn.

syntax_12600.creator: J__00
syntax_12600.title: Item Frame - is Fixed
syntax_12600.description:
Returns whether the item frame is "fixed" or not.
When true it's not possible to destroy/move the frame (e. g. by damage, interaction, pistons, or missing supporting blocks), rotate the item or place/remove items.
syntax_12600.syntax_pattern:
item[ |-]frame %entities% (is|are) fixed
item[ |-]frame %entities% (isn't|is not|aren't|are not) fixed

en:
Returns whether the item frame is "fixed" or not.
When true it's not possible to destroy/move the frame (e. g. by damage, interaction, pistons, or missing supporting blocks), rotate the item or place/remove items.

syntax_12601.creator: J__00
syntax_12601.title: Item Frame - is Visible / is Invisible

%blocks/blockstates% (isn't|is not|aren't|are not) playing [[a] ([music] disc|record)]

en: Checks if a jukebox is currently playing a music disc.

syntax_12603.creator: J__00
syntax_12603.title: Projectile - has Left The Shooter
syntax_12603.description:
Gets whether the provided projectiles have left the hitbox of their shooter and can now hit entities.
This is recalculated each tick if the projectile has a shooter.
syntax_12603.syntax_pattern:
%projectiles% (has|have) [already] left [the[ir]|its] shooter
%projectiles% (doesn't|does not|do not|don't) have [already] left [the[ir]|its] shooter

en:
Gets whether the provided projectiles have left the hitbox of their shooter and can now hit entities.
This is recalculated each tick if the projectile has a shooter.

syntax_12606.title: on Inventory Slot Drop - Drop Info
syntax_12606.description: Checks if the item is being dropped from the cursor or the inventory, if the whole stack is being dropped or both.
syntax_12606.syntax_pattern:
[the] item is[( not|n't)] [being|getting] dropped from the (cursor|inventory)
[the] (whole|full) stack is[( not|n't)] [being|getting] dropped [from the (cursor|inventory)]

en: Checks if the item is being dropped from the cursor or the inventory, if the whole stack is being dropped or both.

syntax_12607.creator: J__00
syntax_12607.title: Anvil GUI - Close
syntax_12607.description:
Closes an anvil gui.
*NOTES*:
- Won't do anything if the anvil gui is not open.
- Must be used before opening another anvil gui for a player.

en:
Closes an anvil gui.
*NOTES*:
- Won't do anything if the anvil gui is not open.
- Must be used before opening another anvil gui for a player.

syntax_12608.creator: J__00
syntax_12608.title: Anvil GUI - Open
syntax_12608.description:
Opens an anvil GUI to one or more players.
*NOTES*:
- Anvil GUIs are always opened 1 tick later.
- You can only open an anvil GUI for a player if the player is not already viewing another (or this) anvil GUI.
- Using `force` will forcibly close the anvil GUI the player is currently viewing to open a new one. Be aware that, due to how anvil GUIs work, closing an anvil GUI is global, meaning it will be closed for all players viewing it. To avoid this, ensure that you create per-player anvil GUIs.

en:
Opens an anvil GUI to one or more players.
*NOTES*:
- Anvil GUIs are always opened 1 tick later.
- You can only open an anvil GUI for a player if the player is not already viewing another (or this) anvil GUI.
- Using `force` will forcibly close the anvil GUI the player is currently viewing to open a new one. Be aware that, due to how anvil GUIs work, closing an anvil GUI is global, meaning it will be closed for all players viewing it. To avoid this, ensure that you create per-player anvil GUIs.

syntax_12609.creator: J__00
syntax_12609.title: Anvil GUI - Prevent Closing
syntax_12609.description:
Prevents an anvil gui from closing.
*NOTES*:
- This effect must be used *BEFORE* the gui is opened; if you need to use it after it's already opened you'll have to open it again.
- If this effect is used you'll need to use the `Anvil GUI - Close` effect to close the gui.

en:
Prevents an anvil gui from closing.
*NOTES*:
- This effect must be used *BEFORE* the gui is opened; if you need to use it after it's already opened you'll have to open it again.
- If this effect is used you'll need to use the `Anvil GUI - Close` effect to close the gui.

syntax_12622.creator: J__00
syntax_12622.title: Player - Sleep
syntax_12622.description:
Attempts to, or forces one or more players to sleep in a bed.
Attempting will fail under vanilla conditions in which the player cannot sleep, ex: Daytime, too far away, etc
syntax_12622.syntax_pattern:
(forcefully|(try|attempt) to) make %players% sleep at %location%
try making %players% sleep at %location%
force %players% to sleep at %location%

en:
Attempts to, or forces one or more players to sleep in a bed.
Attempting will fail under vanilla conditions in which the player cannot sleep, ex: Daytime, too far away, etc

syntax_12623.creator: J__00
syntax_12623.title: Player - Start/Stop Riptiding
syntax_12623.description:
Makes a player riptide. (also known as spin attack)

Stopping makes the player riptide for a tick, which stops it a tick later.

The duration must be greater than or equal to 1 tick.
Attack strength will default to 0

`if player is riptiding:` will return true for the given duration.
syntax_12623.syntax_pattern:
make %players% (start riptiding|riptide|spin attack) for %timespan% [using %itemtype%] [with [attack] (strength|damage) %number%]
make %players% (stop|finish) (riptiding|spin attacking)

en:
Makes a player riptide. (also known as spin attack)

Stopping makes the player riptide for a tick, which stops it a tick later.

The duration must be greater than or equal to 1 tick.
Attack strength will default to 0

`if player is riptiding:` will return true for the given duration.

syntax_9076.title: Fox - Properties
syntax_9076.description:
Various fox properties.
Everything in this expression can be used with `Paper`.
With `Spigot` you can only:
 - `Get` and `Set` the Crouching State and Sleeping State
 - `Get` the Faceplanted State
syntax_9076.syntax_pattern:

%livingentity%'[s] fox [is] (leaping|crouching|defending|sleeping|face[ ]planted) state
whether [the] fox %livingentity% is (leaping|crouching|defending|sleeping|face[ ]planted) [or not]
whether [or not] [the] fox %livingentity% is (leaping|crouching|defending|sleeping|face[ ]planted)

en:
Various fox properties.
Everything in this expression can be used with `Paper`.
With `Spigot` you can only:
 - `Get` and `Set` the Crouching State and Sleeping State

syntax_12640.creator: J__00
syntax_12640.title: Arrow - Damage
syntax_12640.description:
Gets the base amount of damage one or more arrows will do.
Defaults to 2.0 for a normal arrow with 0.5 * (1 + power level) added for arrows fired from enchanted bows.
Can be set, added to, removed from, deleted (set to 0) and reset (set to 2, the default value).

en:
Gets the base amount of damage one or more arrows will do.
Defaults to 2.0 for a normal arrow with 0.5 * (1 + power level) added for arrows fired from enchanted bows.
Can be set, added to, removed from, deleted (set to 0) and reset (set to 2, the default value).

syntax_12642.creator: J__00
syntax_12642.title: AxisAngle/Quaternion/Vector(2D/3D/4D)/Location/EulerAngle/Chunk - XYZ(W) Coordinate/Coordinate List
syntax_12642.description:
Gets a list or one of the coordinates of:
- Locations
- Vectors
- Chunks (only X and Z)
- EulerAngles
- 2D Vectors (only X and Z)
- 3D Vectors
- 4D Vectors (XYZW)
- Quaternions (XYZW)
- AxisAngles (XYZW)
- MapCursor

Note: Out of all of these Lusk only implements EulerAngles.

en:
Gets a list or one of the coordinates of:

- EulerAngles
- 2D Vectors (only X and Z)

- 4D Vectors (XYZW)
- Quaternions (XYZW)
- AxisAngles (XYZW)
- MapCursor

Note: Out of all of these Lusk only implements EulerAngles.

A bell will typically resonate for 40 ticks (2 seconds)

Note: if you wish to use this expression on Skript 2.9+ you will need to use Lusk 1.3+.

syntax_12645.title: Block/BlockData/BlockState/Item - Slipperiness
syntax_12645.description:

Blocks with higher slipperiness, like ice can be slid on further by the player and other entities.
Most blocks have a default slipperiness of 0.6.

Can be used with items, blocks, blockstates, and blockdatas.

syntax_11906.creator: J__00
syntax_11906.title: Fox - Trusted Players
syntax_11906.description:
Returns the first, second, or both trusted players of 1 or more foxes.

*NOTES*:
- The first and second trusted players can both be set to a single `offline player`, the trusted players can be set to 2 offline players (or 1, which only sets the first one).
- The second trusted player must be set after the first one, which also means you can't clear the first one if the second one is set, both actions will fail silently.
- If you wish to set/clear both, do so by using the second pattern: `clear trusted players of {_fox}`
syntax_11906.syntax_pattern:
[the] ([first|second] trusted player|trusted players) of %livingentities%
%livingentities%'[s] ([first|second] trusted player|trusted players)

en:
Returns the first, second, or both trusted players of 1 or more foxes.

*NOTES*:
- The first and second trusted players can both be set to a single `offline player`, the trusted players can be set to 2 offline players (or 1, which only sets the first one).
- The second trusted player must be set after the first one, which also means you can't clear the first one if the second one is set, both actions will fail silently.
- If you wish to set/clear both, do so by using the second pattern: `clear trusted players of {_fox}`

syntax_8781.creator: J__00
syntax_8781.title: On Pre Damage Event
syntax_8781.description:
This event requires Paper.

Called when the player tries to attack an entity. This occurs before any of the damage logic, so cancelling this event will prevent any sort of sounds from being played when attacking. This event will fire as cancelled for certain entities, use the "will be damaged" condition to check if the entity will not actually be attacked.
Note: there may be other factors (invulnerability, etc) that will prevent this entity from being attacked that this event will not cover.

en:
This event requires Paper.

Called when the player tries to attack an entity. This occurs before any of the damage logic, so cancelling this event will prevent any sort of sounds from being played when attacking. This event will fire as cancelled for certain entities, use the "will be damaged" condition to check if the entity will not actually be attacked.
Note: there may be other factors (invulnerability, etc) that will prevent this entity from being attacked that this event will not cover.

syntax_8771.creator: J__00

syntax_12685.creator: J__00
syntax_12685.title: Item Frame - Item Drop Chance
syntax_12685.description:
The chance of the item being dropped upon this item frame's destruction.
Can be set, removed from, added to, reset (1) and deleted (0).

The final value is always clamped between 0 and 1.

1 = always drops; 0 = never drops.
syntax_12685.syntax_pattern:
[the] item[ |-]frame [item] drop chance of %entities%
%entities%'[s] item[ |-]frame [item] drop chance

en:
The chance of the item being dropped upon this item frame's destruction.
Can be set, removed from, added to, reset (1) and deleted (0).

The final value is always clamped between 0 and 1.

1 = always drops; 0 = never drops.

syntax_8847.creator: J__00
syntax_8847.title: Warden - Anger

Returns the anger of a warden.

Angry = 80 or above
Agitated = 40-79
Calm = 0-39

Use the respective conditions to check for those 3 states.

Returns the anger of a warden.

syntax_11909.creator: J__00
syntax_11909.title: Used Tool/Used Hand/Used Equipment Slot
syntax_11909.description:
Retrieves the used hand or item used in events where either hand is used.
The first pattern returns the Slot (Item + Index) of the item in the used hand.
- Note: using this is not the same as using `event-slot` or `event-item`, if either is available, use that
The second pattern returns an Equipment Slot that's either `hand_slot` or `off_hand_slot`.

Supported Events:
- on Right Click
- on Item Consume
- on Arm Swing
- on Entity Shoot (different than 'on Shoot'!)
- on Crossbow Load
- on Place (includes 'on Block Place', 'on Hanging Place', and 'on Entity Place')
- on Resurrect
- on Player Fish
- on Bucket Fill
- on Bucket Empty
- on Elytra Boost
- on Player Harvest
- on Can Build Check
- on Player Shear Entity
- on Player Leash Entity
- on Player Unleash Entity
- on Player Use Unknown Entity

en:

syntax_12665.creator: J__00
syntax_12665.title: Entity - Collidable Exemptions
syntax_12665.description:
Gets the list of entities (or their UUIDs) which are exempt from the provided entities' collidable rule and whose collision with this entity will behave the opposite of it.
Can be set, added to, removed from, and deleted.

For example if collidable is true and an entity is in the exemptions then it will not collide with it.
Similarly if collidable is false and an entity is in this set then it will still collide with it.

Note: **these exemptions are not (currently) persistent.**

Note: **the client may predict the collision between itself and another entity, resulting in those exemptions not being accurate for player collisions.**
 **This expression should therefore only be used to exempt non-player entities.**
 **To exempt collisions for a player, use the Team Collision Rule Option in combination with a Scoreboard and a Team.**

en:
Gets the list of entities (or their UUIDs) which are exempt from the provided entities' collidable rule and whose collision with this entity will behave the opposite of it.
Can be set, added to, removed from, and deleted.

For example if collidable is true and an entity is in the exemptions then it will not collide with it.
Similarly if collidable is false and an entity is in this set then it will still collide with it.

Note: **these exemptions are not (currently) persistent.**

Note: **the client may predict the collision between itself and another entity, resulting in those exemptions not being accurate for player collisions.**
 **This expression should therefore only be used to exempt non-player entities.**
 **To exempt collisions for a player, use the Team Collision Rule Option in combination with a Scoreboard and a Team.**

syntax_11862.title: On Armor Stand - on Manipulate
syntax_11862.description:
Called when a player interacts with an armor stand and will either swap, retrieve, place an item.

`event-equipmentslot` is the clicked slot of the armor stand, to get the hand use the Used Hand expression
`event-entity` is the armor stand

`past event-item` is the item the armor stand is holding
`future event-item` (or `event-item`) is the item the player is holding

`event-armorstand interaction` is the type of manipulation that's happening during the event
(NOTE: this will only ever return `Place`, `Retrieve` or `Change`)
syntax_11862.event_values: event-armor stand interaction, event-entity, event-equipment slot, event-item stack, event-item type, event-living entity, event-player, event-world, future event-item type

Called when a player interacts with an armor stand and will either swap, retrieve, place an item.

`event-equipmentslot` is the clicked slot of the armor stand, to get the hand use the Used Hand expression
`event-entity` is the armor stand

`past event-item` is the item the armor stand is holding
`future event-item` (or `event-item`) is the item the player is holding

`event-armorstand interaction` is the type of manipulation that's happening during the event
(NOTE: this will only ever return `Place`, `Retrieve` or `Change`)

syntax_12567.creator: J__00
syntax_12567.title: On Block - on Ignite [Lusk Extension]
syntax_12567.description:
**THIS IS AN EXTENDED VERSION OF SKRIPT'S IGNITE EVENT.**

`event-player` = the Player that placed/ignited the fire block, or not set if not ignited by a Player.

`event-entity` = the Entity that placed/ignited the fire block, or not set if not ignited by an Entity.

`event-ignitecause` = the value detailing the cause of the block ignition.

Called when a block is ignited. If you want to catch when a Player places fire, use the place event.
If this event is cancelled, the block will not be ignited.
syntax_12567.event_values: event-block, event-entity, event-ignition cause, event-location, event-player, event-world

en:
**THIS IS AN EXTENDED VERSION OF SKRIPT'S IGNITE EVENT.**

`event-player` = the Player that placed/ignited the fire block, or not set if not ignited by a Player.

`event-entity` = the Entity that placed/ignited the fire block, or not set if not ignited by an Entity.

`event-ignitecause` = the value detailing the cause of the block ignition.

Called when a block is ignited. If you want to catch when a Player places fire, use the place event.
If this event is cancelled, the block will not be ignited.

Hopper
Detector rails
Nether portals
Powdered snow
Pressure plates
Sweet berry bush
Tripwire

Web
Wither rose

syntax_12588.creator: J__00
syntax_12588.title: Entity - Should Burn In Day
syntax_12588.description:
Returns whether or not the provided entities should burn during the day.
Applies to zombies, skeletons (all types), and phantoms

In 1.17.1 this doesn't work with skeleton variants, such as strays, but it does work with base skeletons.

For skeletons, this does not take into account the entity's natural fire immunity.
syntax_12588.syntax_pattern:
[[living[ |-]]entity] %livingentities% should burn ((during|in) [the] day|(in|under) [the] (sun[light]|daylight))
[[living[ |-]]entity] %livingentities% (should not|shouldn't) burn ((during|in) [the] day|(in|under) [the] (sun[light]|daylight))

en:
Returns whether or not the provided entities should burn during the day.
Applies to zombies, skeletons (all types), and phantoms

In 1.17.1 this doesn't work with skeleton variants, such as strays, but it does work with base skeletons.

For skeletons, this does not take into account the entity's natural fire immunity.

syntax_13012.creator: J__00
syntax_13012.title: Block - Liquid Level
syntax_13012.description:
Represents the maximum, minimum or current amount of fluid contained within a block, either by itself or inside a cauldron.
Minimum Level requires Paper and 1.18.2.

This expression includes liquid blocks, cauldron and composters.

For water and lava blocks the levels have special meanings: a level of 0 corresponds to a source block, 1-7 regular fluid heights, and 8-15 to "falling" fluids.
All falling fluids have the same behaviour, but the level corresponds to that of the block above them, equal to this. level - 8

**Note that counterintuitively, an adjusted level of 1 is the highest level, whilst 7 is the lowest.**

May not be higher than the max level.

Can be set, added to, removed from and reset.
Reset requires Paper and 1.18.2.

en:

syntax_13039.creator: J__00
syntax_13039.title: Entity - on Resurrect Section
syntax_13039.description:
Runs the code inside of it when the provided entity dies and may have the opportunity to be resurrected.
Will be called in a cancelled state if the entity does not have a totem equipped.

Local Variables that are:
- defined BEFORE this section CAN be used inside of it.
- defined AFTER this section CANNOT be used inside of it.
- defined INSIDE this section CANNOT be used outside of it.
syntax_13039.syntax_pattern:
[execute|run] on [entity] resurrect[ion] [attempt] of %entity%
[execute|run] when %entity% (resurrect[s]|attempt[s] to resurrect)

en:
Runs the code inside of it when the provided entity dies and may have the opportunity to be resurrected.
Will be called in a cancelled state if the entity does not have a totem equipped.

Local Variables that are:
- defined BEFORE this section CAN be used inside of it.
- defined AFTER this section CANNOT be used inside of it.
- defined INSIDE this section CANNOT be used outside of it.

en:
Send an experience change. This fakes an experience change packet for a user. This will not actually change the experience points in any way.
Progress must be within 0 and 1.

syntax_14006.creator: J__00
syntax_14006.title: On Entity - on Collide With Block
syntax_14006.description:
Called when an entity enters the hitbox of a block. Only called for blocks that react when an entity is inside. If cancelled, any action that would have resulted from that entity being in the block will not happen (such as extinguishing an entity in a cauldron).
Blocks this is currently called for:

Big dripleaf
Bubble column
Buttons
Cactus
Campfire
Cauldron
Crops
Ender Portal
Fires
Frogspawn
Honey
Hopper
Detector rails
Nether portals
Powdered snow
Pressure plates
Sweet berry bush
Tripwire
Waterlily
Web
Wither rose
syntax_14006.event_values: event-block, event-command sender, event-entity, event-entity type, event-location, event-world

en:
Called when an entity enters the hitbox of a block. Only called for blocks that react when an entity is inside. If cancelled, any action that would have resulted from that entity being in the block will not happen (such as extinguishing an entity in a cauldron).

syntax_14012.title: On Entity - on Push by Entity Attack
syntax_14012.description:
Fired when an entity is pushed by another entity's attack.
The acceleration vector can be modified. If this event is cancelled, the entity will not get pushed.

Note: Some entities might trigger this multiple times on the same entity as multiple acceleration calculations are done.
syntax_14012.event_values: event-command sender, event-entity, event-entity type, event-location, event-vector, event-world

en:
Fired when an entity is pushed by another entity's attack.
The acceleration vector can be modified. If this event is cancelled, the entity will not get pushed.

Note: Some entities might trigger this multiple times on the same entity as multiple acceleration calculations are done.

A 'harvest' is when a block drops an item (usually some sort of crop) and changes state, but is not broken in order to drop the item.
This event is not called for when a block is broken.

syntax_12568.creator: J__00
syntax_12568.title: On Player - on Interact
syntax_12568.description:
Called when a player interacts with a block or entity by clicking.

This event is always called unlike Skript's click event, meaning that it can be called for both hands.

You can use `main hand` and `off hand` to make it per hand.

`event-equipmentslot` = the slot of the used hand
`event-blockaction` = the action of this event
`event-vector` = the vector from the player to the interaction point
`event-location` = the location of the interaction point
syntax_12568.event_values: event-block, event-block action, event-block face, event-direction, event-entity, event-equipment slot, event-item stack, event-location, event-player, event-vector, event-world

syntax_14022.creator: J__00
syntax_14022.title: On Player - on Pre Damage
syntax_14022.description:
Called when the player tries to attack an entity. This occurs before any of the damage logic, so cancelling this event will prevent any sort of sounds from being played when attacking. This event will fire as cancelled for certain entities, use the "will be damaged" condition to check if the entity will not actually be attacked.
Note: there may be other factors (invulnerability, etc) that will prevent this entity from being attacked that this event will not cover.

en:
Called when the player tries to attack an entity. This occurs before any of the damage logic, so cancelling this event will prevent any sort of sounds from being played when attacking. This event will fire as cancelled for certain entities, use the "will be damaged" condition to check if the entity will not actually be attacked.
Note: there may be other factors (invulnerability, etc) that will prevent this entity from being attacked that this event will not cover.
